controls.title: Security Management Process (§164.308(a)(1))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations. Includes risk analysis and risk management.
evidence: Risk analysis documentation covering ePHI systems
Risk management plan and treatment decisions

sub_controls.title: Risk Analysis (Required) — Conduct accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks to ePHI
Risk Management (Required) — Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks to a reasonable and appropriate level
Sanction Policy (Required) — Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply
Information System Activity Review (Required) — Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity

controls.title: Assigned Security Responsibility (§164.308(a)(2))
controls.description: Identify the security official responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by this subpart.
evidence: Documented appointment of Security Officer role
Security Officer responsibilities and accountability records

sub_controls.title: Security Official (Required) — Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of policies and procedures

controls.title: Workforce Security (§164.308(a)(3))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to ensure all members of the workforce have appropriate access to ePHI and to prevent those who do not have access from obtaining it.
evidence: Clearance and onboarding access provisioning records
Offboarding / access termination procedures and evidence

sub_controls.title: Authorization and/or Supervision (Addressable) — Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members
Workforce Clearance Procedure (Addressable) — Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to ePHI is appropriate
Termination Procedures (Addressable) — Implement procedures for terminating access to ePHI

controls.title: Information Access Management (§164.308(a)(4))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to ePHI consistent with the minimum necessary standard.
evidence: Access authorization policy and approval workflow

sub_controls.title: Isolating Health Care Clearinghouse Functions (Required) — Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI maintained by a health care clearinghouse
Access Authorization (Addressable) — Implement policies and procedures for granting access to ePHI

Access Establishment and Modification (Addressable) — Implement policies and procedures that establish, document, review, and modify user access rights to a workstation, transaction, program, or process

controls.title: Security Awareness and Training (§164.308(a)(5))
controls.description: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of the workforce, including management.

sub_controls.title: Security Reminders (Addressable) — Periodic security updates
Protection from Malicious Software (Addressable) — Procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software
Log-in Monitoring (Addressable) — Procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies
Password Management (Addressable) — Procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords

controls.title: Security Incident Procedures (§164.308(a)(6))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents, including response and reporting of security incidents.
evidence: Incident response plan covering ePHI breaches

sub_controls.title: Response and Reporting (Required) — Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents

controls.title: Contingency Plan (§164.308(a)(7))
controls.description: Establish policies and procedures for responding to an emergency that damages systems containing ePHI, including data backup, disaster recovery, and emergency mode operation.
evidence: Data backup plan and backup execution logs
Disaster recovery plan for ePHI systems
Business continuity / emergency mode operation plan

sub_controls.title: Data Backup Plan (Required) — Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI
Disaster Recovery Plan (Required) — Establish procedures to restore any loss of data
Emergency Mode Operation Plan (Required) — Establish procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes
Testing and Revision Procedures (Addressable) — Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans
Applications and Data Criticality Analysis (Addressable) — Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data

controls.title: Evaluation (§164.308(a)(8))
controls.description: Perform periodic technical and non-technical evaluations to assess the extent to which security policies and procedures meet the requirements of this subpart.
evidence: Periodic security assessment or audit reports
Vulnerability scan and penetration test results

sub_controls.title: Evaluation (Required) — Perform a periodic technical and non-technical evaluation in response to environmental or operational changes

controls.title: Facility Access Controls (§164.310(a))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and the facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.
evidence: Badge and key access logs for server rooms / data centers

sub_controls.title: Contingency Operations (Addressable) — Establish and implement procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data
Facility Security Plan (Addressable) — Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and equipment
Access Control and Validation Procedures (Addressable) — Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities
Maintenance Records (Addressable) — Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components

controls.title: Workstation Use and Security (§164.310(b)/(c))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures specifying proper functions performed on workstations that access ePHI, and physical safeguards for workstations.

sub_controls.title: Workstation Use (Required) — Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings
Workstation Security (Required) — Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI

controls.title: Device and Media Controls (§164.310(d))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI, and movement of these items within the facility.
evidence: DLP policy covering removable media and ePHI

sub_controls.title: Disposal (Required) — Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of ePHI and/or the hardware or electronic media
Media Re-use (Required) — Implement procedures for removal of ePHI from electronic media before it is made available for re-use
Accountability (Addressable) — Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media
Data Backup and Storage (Addressable) — Create a retrievable exact copy of ePHI before movement of equipment

controls.title: Access Control (§164.312(a))
controls.description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights.
evidence: Unique user identifier policy and implementation evidence

sub_controls.title: Unique User Identification (Required) — Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity
Emergency Access Procedure (Required) — Establish and implement procedures for obtaining necessary ePHI during an emergency
Automatic Logoff (Addressable) — Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity
Encryption and Decryption (Addressable) — Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI

controls.title: Audit Controls (§164.312(b))
controls.description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.
evidence: Audit logging configuration for all ePHI systems
SIEM alert rules for ePHI access anomalies

sub_controls.title: Audit Controls (Required) — Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI

controls.title: Integrity Controls (§164.312(c))
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction, and ensure it has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.
evidence: DLP policy rules covering ePHI modification controls

sub_controls.title: Mechanism to Authenticate ePHI (Addressable) — Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner

controls.title: Person or Entity Authentication (§164.312(d))
controls.description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.
evidence: MFA configuration for all ePHI system access
Evidence of strong password or passwordless controls

sub_controls.title: Person or Entity Authentication (Required) — Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed

controls.title: Transmission Security (§164.312(e))
controls.description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.
evidence: Encryption-in-transit configuration for all ePHI transmissions
TLS certificate inventory and expiry monitoring
VPN or encrypted channel configuration for remote access
Scan results confirming no unencrypted ePHI transmission

sub_controls.title: Integrity Controls (Addressable) — Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified
Encryption (Addressable) — Implement a mechanism to encrypt ePHI in transit
